title: Steeves Bujold, Ad. E.
content:
## Steeves Bujold, Ad. E.
Partner | Chief Inclusion Officer
People.ContactEmail sbujold@mccarthy.ca
A first-rate litigator, Steeves is regularly called upon to defend and resolve the most complex disputes.
Steeves Bujold, Ad. E. is a partner in the Montréal office and the firm’s Chief Inclusion Officer. He served as the leader of the Litigation Group for the Quebec region from 2017 to 2024. He has a diversified litigation practice as well as cutting-edge expertise in health law, medical and pharmaceutical liability, and professional law. He regularly appears before the courts, as well as before the courts of appeal and administrative tribunals.
He was the 94 th President of the Canadian Bar Association (2022-2023) and was a member of its Board of Directors from 2019 to 2023. At the CBA, he also served as Vice-Chair (2021-2022), Chair of the Equality Subcommittee (2019-2020), Chair of the CEO Selection Committee, and Chair of the Policy Committee (2020-2021). He is a member of the Motions Committee of the Barreau du Québec.
He served as the first President of the Pride Action Group and, in this capacity, the national leader of diversity and inclusion initiatives for the 2SLGBTQ+ community. During his tenure as the 94 th President of the Canadian Bar Association (2022-2023), and as the first person with a same-sex spouse to hold this position, he was a fervent advocate for the rights of 2SLGBTQ+ community members in Canada and abroad. He is an officer on the LGBTQI+ Law Committee of the International Bar Association (IBA).
Mr. Bujold is currently a member of the Advisory Committee on Federal Judicial Appointments for the Province of Quebec West for a 2nd term and was a member of the Independent Advisory Board for Appointments to the Supreme Court of Canada.
In 2023, the Barreau du Québec awarded him the Advocatus Emeritus (Ad. E.) distinction, an honorary title awarded to its members who distinguish themselves by the excellence of their professional careers, their exceptional contribution to the profession and their influence in their social and community environment. Mr. Bujold is also a recipient of the King Charles III Coronation Medal and the Queen Elizabeth II Diamond Jubilee Medal. He is also Governor Emeritus of the Fondation du Barreau du Québec.
He obtained a bachelor's degree in civil law from Université Laval in 1998. He was admitted to the Quebec Bar in 1999 and to the Ordre des administrateurs agréés du Québec in 2021.
